summary: High-signal product and tool mentions mined from 25 scraped front-page stories, led by AI releases and developer tooling.
telegram_footer: 10 items surfaced | 25/25 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI's latest model release with prompt-length and token-spend debate.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as the main model drop and compared it directly with Codex and Claude Code.
items.evidence: Replies compared GPT-5.6 with Codex and Claude Code, and debated whether shorter prompts really save tokens.

items.name: ChatGPT Work
items.why_included: The thread made the product shift concrete by focusing on how OpenAI merged the work and coding surfaces.
items.evidence: Commenters focused on the confusing split between ChatGPT Work and ChatGPT Codex, calling the app change hard to follow.

items.name: Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta's model API release pitched at lower-cost inference.
items.why_included: Readers compared its pricing and capability directly with other frontier model APIs.
items.evidence: Discussion compared Muse Spark 1.1 with Grok 4.5 and highlighted the cached-input price/performance tradeoff.

items.name: Pangram
items.source_story_title: AI content is everywhere on social media, especially LinkedIn
items.summary: An AI text detector and browser extension for written content.
items.why_included: It surfaced as a concrete AI-detection product with direct praise from commenters.
items.evidence: One commenter said 'Pangram does work' and described it as useful for spotting AI-written text.

items.name: Colibri
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Getting GLM 5.2 running on my slow computer
items.summary: A lightweight local runner for GLM 5.2 on slow computers.
items.why_included: The repo got a positive signal from a benchmark-oriented comment on the HN thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said the readme benchmarks looked great across different hardware and reported good times on the Colibri runs.

items.name: Context.dev
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Context.dev (YC S26) – API to get structured data from any website
items.summary: An API that turns websites into structured data and screenshots.
items.why_included: The launch thread produced a strong positive user report and a concrete alternative suggestion.
items.evidence: A user said Context.dev was a great product that made a hard problem disappear, then recommended Intercept as an alternative.

items.name: Intercept
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Context.dev (YC S26) – API to get structured data from any website
items.summary: A typed proxy API that maps the real transport layer of websites.
items.why_included: It was recommended outright as a possible replacement for scraping-heavy workflows.
items.evidence: One commenter said "Have a look at Intercept" and described it as a typed TypeScript proxy API that reverse-engineers website traffic.

items.name: acme-dns
items.source_story_title: TLS certificates for internal services done right
items.summary: An ACME DNS helper for issuing certs to internal services.
items.why_included: The thread contained a direct real-world endorsement from someone using it in production.
items.evidence: A commenter said, "I do this exactly, using ACME DNS," in the internal certificate discussion.

items.name: Paperclip
items.source_story_title: Show HN: I mapped 8.5M research papers into an interactive atlas
items.summary: An agent-first CLI for scientific literature and evidence.
items.why_included: The project drew useful feedback around search, filtering, and research workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters called the atlas neat, asked for search-term filtering, and described it as useful for exploring a corpus.

items.name: Analog Watch
items.summary: An interactive analog watch game that trains clock reading.
items.why_included: It got straightforward positive playtesting feedback and looked like a polished side project.
items.evidence: Replies called it fun and said the practice rounds were needed to stay sharp.
